debug_points.description: This location appears to be captured from above a body of water, likely a pond or lake. The images show murky green-brown water with grass pavers (turf block/grid pavers) visible along the water's edge in some directions. No road surface is visible in any of the 9 images - this is not a street location.

The images show a flooded street with standing water covering most of the road surface. While water is pooling extensively, there is no visible evidence of actual potholes - no exposed base material, no jagged broken edges, no visible depth or holes where asphalt is missing. The water obscures the road surface but does not reveal any structural damage beneath.

These images show a parking lot or paved area with marked parking spaces (yellow number '3' and yellow line visible). The surface appears to be concrete/asphalt that is weathered but intact. The large gray area visible in most images appears to be standing water or a wet surface reflection, not a pothole - there are no visible holes, broken edges, exposed base material, or missing asphalt anywhere in the frames.

The camera appears to be positioned over or very close to a body of water, likely a pond or water hazard. All images show murky green-brown water surface with no road visible. Images 1, 7, and 8 show grass edges with wooden bollards/posts marking the water boundary. Images 4-6 show what appears to be an asphalt surface or pathway at the edge of the water feature.

Large puddle of standing water covering most of the roadway, but no visible pothole edges, exposed base material, or depth indicators can be seen through or around the water. The water appears to be pooling on a relatively flat asphalt surface near a yellow-painted curb.

The images show a very large body of murky water (appears to be a flooded area or pond) adjacent to a paved road with grass and vegetation along the edges. The road surface visible in images 5, 6, 7, and 8 appears to be worn asphalt but shows no actual potholes with missing material. The water is opaque and no road surface or holes can be seen beneath it.

The camera appears to be positioned in or very near a body of water, likely a canal or waterway adjacent to a road. The murky greenish-brown water dominates most views. Where visible, there is a concrete walkway/path with grass embankment (image 6), and asphalt road surface (images 2, 3). No potholes are visible in the road sections shown.

The images show a road surface with standing water/puddle covering most of the viewable area. While there is extensive water pooling, there is no visible evidence of actual potholes - no exposed base material, no broken edges, no visible depth or holes with missing asphalt can be seen through or around the water. The road appears to have minor surface wear but no structural damage creating holes.

The images show a road surface with significant standing water/flooding covering most of the visible area. The camera appears to be positioned very low, possibly at ground level or from a vehicle in flooded conditions. No actual potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or broken edges are visible - only water covering the road surface and curb/grass edges visible at the periphery.

The camera appears to be positioned over or very close to a body of water, likely a canal, river, or flooded area. All images show murky greenish-grey water surface with no visible road pavement. Image 6 (Southwest) shows what appears to be a road edge/embankment with a yellow post marker, asphalt road surface, and rocky/vegetated bank beyond the water.

These images appear to be taken from a position over water, likely a bridge or waterfront area. The murky brown-grey area dominating most frames is water, not road surface. Visible road sections show concrete sidewalk/curb (images 1, 7, 8) and asphalt road (images 4, 5, 6, 7) with no potholes present.

The images show a roadside area with a paved road and adjacent unpaved/gravel areas. There is significant water pooling or flooding visible in most images, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or visible depth can be confirmed. The murky brown water obscures the ground surface completely, making it impossible to verify any holes beneath.

This is an aerial view from a construction site/tall building overlooking an urban intersection. The road surfaces visible show normal asphalt with typical wear patterns, road markings, and crosswalks. No potholes or holes with missing asphalt are visible in any direction.

The images show a flooded roadway with extensive standing water covering most of the road surface. While there is significant water pooling visible in multiple directions, no actual potholes with visible depth, broken edges, or exposed base material can be confirmed. The yellow painted curb is visible in several images indicating the road edge near a sidewalk area.

Images show a large area of standing water/puddle on what appears to be a paved surface with visible concrete/asphalt edges around the perimeter. The water completely obscures the road surface beneath it, making it impossible to confirm whether any actual potholes (holes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or depth) exist underneath. No broken edges, exposed aggregate, or visible depressions can be seen through or around the water.

The images show a large body of murky water (likely a canal, waterway, or flooded area) with a gravel/dirt shoreline or roadway edge visible at the top of several frames. The camera appears to be positioned over or very close to water, not on a typical road surface. No asphalt road surface is visible, and therefore no potholes can be identified.

The images show a road surface with significant standing water or flooding covering most of the visible area. While water is present, there is no visible hole, missing asphalt, exposed base material, or broken edges that would indicate an actual pothole. The visible asphalt portions appear intact with normal surface texture and road markings.

These images appear to be taken from an unusual angle, possibly from a vehicle-mounted camera that is tilted downward or malfunctioning. Most frames show either sky/dark gray areas or partial views of a blue vehicle. The visible road surface in images 5-7 shows weathered concrete or asphalt with surface wear and discoloration but no visible potholes with missing material or exposed base layers.

These images show a large body of murky brown/grey water with concrete or asphalt edges visible at the periphery. This appears to be either a flooded area, a large puddle on a road surface, or a body of water adjacent to pavement. No actual potholes with visible depth, broken edges, or exposed base material can be confirmed - the water completely obscures any potential road damage beneath it.

The images show a paved road surface with standing water/puddle covering much of the visible area. While there is significant water pooling, I cannot see any actual holes, missing asphalt, exposed base material, or broken edges beneath the water. The visible road surface appears intact where it can be seen.
